dungeon: GENUINE ROTF — a focused THE PUPPET MASTER boss fight (orange/lava enclosed arena). HIGH taunt yield: a near-full Puppet Master taunt set in boss-coloured chat (upgrades the partial reads from disc_1mU5uHE6DXE). Theatrical 'puppet' boss. Strong ROTF-custom build candidate.

bosses.name: The Puppet Master
phases: taunts through the fight; 'NO!!! This cannot...' on death
attacks: DOCUMENTED-from-frames: clustered/scattered YELLOW-GOLD bullet bursts in the orange arena (tight groupings); the 'Find me if you can hide' theme suggests a hide/teleport or summon component -> reconstruct as aimed bursts + possible Invisible/summon phase

Lucky guess hero, but [...] you. It is time that you died!
bosses.note: DOCUMENTED-from-chat (boss-coloured, clearly Puppet Master). Strong build candidate; for the build use the readable stems + mark cut completions.

mechanics: DOCUMENTED: a single The Puppet Master boss fight in an orange/lava arena. A theatrical PUPPET boss — taunts are taunting/mocking ('Find me if you can hide', 'Lucky guess hero', 'I hope you're ready', 'ENCORE, HERO!') with a death line ('NO!!! This cannot be!'). ATTACK: clustered yellow-gold bullet bursts (DOCUMENTED-from-frames); the 'hide' theme hints at a stealth/teleport or summon mechanic (reconstruct, not confirmed). This UPGRADES the Puppet Master entry from disc_1mU5uHE6DXE (which had only fragmentary reads).
uncertain: Every taunt line is RIGHT-EDGE CUT by the narrow chat crop -> stems are solid, completions ([ide]/[or]/[be]) are likely but NOT confirmed verbatim. Attack geometry approximate (clustered bursts; no clean wide bossends). 'hide/summon' mechanic inferred from the 'Find me if you can hide' line, not seen. Arena small/enclosed, dungeon-of-origin not identified.
confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH. GENUINE ROTF, clearly THE PUPPET MASTER (boss-coloured full-ish taunt set). Taunts DOCUMENTED but right-edge-cut (stems verbatim, completions bracketed). Best Puppet Master source so far (upgrades disc_1mU5uHE6DXE). Strong build/enrich candidate — the auto-bridge should pick it up; for the build, use the verbatim stems and mark the cut completions.
